meta_title: For Students: analytics setup to Solve no marketing skills (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome no marketing skills. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Analytics Setup: Solve No Marketing Skills (Advanced Guide)
content.seo_description: Stop guessing. Use this advanced checklist to master analytics setup using Antigravity and Claude Code to turn student projects into data-driven powerhouses.
content.answer_block: When you lack marketing skills, data is your only compass. By implementing a robust analytics stack, you transform anecdotal feedback into actionable product insights. This guide leverages Antigravity and Claude Code to automate event tracking, user segmentation, and conversion funnel analysis, effectively replacing intuition with high-fidelity, evidence-based development.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks architecting your app, polishing the UI, and deploying to production. Then, silence. Without a marketing background, you're left staring at a ghost town, wondering why your features aren't gaining traction. The problem isn't your code; it's your visibility into user behavior.

True engineering is the pursuit of optimization through observation. By treating your analytics setup as a first-class feature rather than an afterthought, you bridge the gap between 'code that runs' and 'product that scales.' Let’s transform your project from a static portfolio piece into a data-driven machine that tells you exactly where your users are getting stuck.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Metric
steps.description: Identify the single event that signals user success. Without this, your analytics data is just noise. We will define this schema to ensure every subsequent event supports this goal.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current codebase and suggest a 5-event tracking schema focused on user retention and conversion for a SaaS application.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Generate a schema.json file defining core user events for my application'

steps.title: Automate Event Middleware Injection
steps.description: Manual tracking is brittle. Use Claude Code to inject a centralized tracking middleware that hooks into your API endpoints automatically.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a TypeScript middleware for Express/Next.js that captures event names, user IDs, and timestamps and pushes them to my analytics endpoint.
steps.claude_code_command: claude apply --file src/middleware/analytics.ts --pattern 'inject tracking middleware into all POST routes'

steps.title: Implement User Cohort Segmentation
steps.description: Distinguish between power users and churned users. We need to tag sessions to understand behavior patterns across different acquisition channels.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a function that attaches metadata (browser, device, acquisition source) to every tracking event payload.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Update analytics service to include visitor metadata in every event payload'

steps.title: Build Automated Funnel Visualization
steps.description: Visualize where users drop off. We will use Antigravity to generate the SQL queries needed to calculate conversion rates at each stage of your funnel.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a SQL query to calculate the conversion percentage between 'sign_up' and 'first_project_created' events grouped by week.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Create a dashboard.sql file with queries for conversion funnel analysis'

steps.title: Configure Automated Anomaly Alerts
steps.description: Don't wait for a report. Set up a system that alerts you when your conversion rates drop below a specific baseline.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a lightweight monitoring script that checks the last 24 hours of event data and logs a warning if conversion drops 20% below the moving average.
steps.claude_code_command: claude apply --file scripts/monitor.ts --pattern 'add logic for conversion anomaly alerts'

pro_tips: Track events at the database layer, not just the client, to avoid data loss from ad-blockers and network failures.
Tag your deployment versions in your analytics metadata so you can correlate feature releases with conversion spikes or drops.
Prioritize 'Actionable Events' over 'Vanity Metrics'—page views are useless; 'Project Created' is a business outcome.
Use a unique 'Correlation ID' that persists across login sessions to link anonymous browsing to registered user activity.
Schedule a weekly 'Data Review' with yourself; analytics are only valuable if they dictate your next sprint's development focus.
content.next_steps: Now that your tracking is live, allow 7 days of data collection. Once the data flows, use Antigravity to perform a 'gap analysis' on your conversion funnel to identify the exact UI element causing the highest bounce rate.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer just a coder; you are a product engineer. By mastering the feedback loop, you have already surpassed 90% of your peers. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
